transport_contract.authentication: download is public; playlist and storage object use optional host-only session plus exact signed object token; all upload routes use Cap API-key precedence then browser session
transport_contract.body: GET and HEAD are bodyless; POST accepts exact camelCase application/json up to 8388608 bytes with identity content encoding
transport_contract.cache: playlist and storage-object responses retain Cache-Control no-store/no-cache/must-revalidate/proxy-revalidate plus Pragma no-cache and Expires 0
transport_contract.tenant_non_disclosure: foreign, inactive, deleted, wrong-prefix, or unbound video/object/session targets are all projected as 404
transport_contract.idempotency: a supplied 8..128-byte Idempotency-Key is stored only as a domain-separated SHA-256 digest and replays a request-bound result; pinned desktop, recorder-core, CLI, and web clients omit the header, so Frame generates a one-execution key while retaining the immutable operation journal

download_contract.precedence: Windows first, macOS with Intel user-agent refinement second, non-Android Linux third, default Apple Silicon
download_contract.response: 307 same-origin /download/:platform with query and fragment removed

playlist_contract.segments: versioned manifest, at most 10000 video and 10000 audio entries, finite positive durations, requireComplete fence, exact init and zero-padded segment keys, VOD markers only after completion
playlist_contract.auxiliary: raw preview first uses the persisted rawFileKey; webMP4 without that evidence probes nonempty raw-upload.mp4 then raw-upload.webm; transcription.vtt is returned as text/vtt; enhanced-audio.mp3 is redirected
playlist_contract.head: HEAD executes the same authority and object selection as GET and returns identical status and headers without a body

storage_object_contract.token: Cap base64url payload and HMAC-SHA256 signature using FRAME_LEGACY_CORE_STORAGE_NEXTAUTH_SECRET; videoId, key, and expiresAt are exact-bound with a 24-hour future ceiling
storage_object_contract.key: must remain under the persisted historical {legacyOwnerId}/{legacyVideoId}/ prefix; the current Frame UUID never replaces that backfilled prefix
storage_object_contract.range: one RFC bytes range only: start-end, start-, or -suffix; unsatisfiable and multi-range requests return 416; GET and HEAD return the same 200/206 Content-Length and Content-Range
storage_object_contract.content_disposition: not synthesized, matching the pinned proxy's copied-header allowlist

upload_contract.target: videoId/subpath or deprecated fileKey; fileKey actor text is discarded and authenticated ownership is reasserted before the persisted historical prefix is joined
upload_contract.single: one exact R2 S3-compatible PUT signed with content type, approved metadata, expiry, and If-None-Match: *; source method=post is safely projected to the released clients' PUT response shape because R2 POST cannot enforce the required no-overwrite precondition
upload_contract.batch: 1..50 unique normalized subpaths, each receiving an exact immutable PUT; response includes uploads and urls aliases
upload_contract.multipart: R2 provider upload IDs remain D1-only; clients receive UUIDv7 external IDs; part capabilities bind exact key, provider handle, part number, optional MD5, and expiry
upload_contract.free_plan_completion: the active organization owner's Pro-seat authority is reasserted; free-plan raw recorder uploads require duration, and any reported duration above 330 seconds is rejected before a completion intent is admitted, with journaled best-effort multipart abort cleanup
upload_contract.metadata: finite string-or-number duration, width, height, and fps update only the owner-scoped native video
upload_contract.governance: each capability operation creates one immutable object intent with tenant, native/legacy video identity, integration, content type, role, requested method, and operation receipt; repeated operations may reissue the same exact no-overwrite key and do not claim that bytes exist

provider_gate_contract.multipart_complete: ordered immutable part evidence, total bytes, parts digest, exact session binding, and effect_pending operation are committed atomically; no R2 complete call occurs until provider_execution evidence is admitted
provider_gate_contract.recording_complete: desktopMP4 returns the exact success/already-complete response; desktopSegments creates one immutable provider_pending finalization intent and returns 503 provider_execution; retries resume that video-bound intent, and unsupported sources return 400
provider_gate_contract.cloudflare_media: the retained source queues remux/transcode workflows; Frame records the exact provider intent but does not claim Cloudflare Media execution, callback, output verification, or billing reconciliation

durability_contract.operations: immutable source/actor/idempotency/request binding with claimed -> effect_pending|complete and effect_pending -> complete transitions
durability_contract.multipart: open -> completion_pending -> complete or open -> abort_pending -> aborted; tenant, prefix, key, provider handle, and evidence cannot change
durability_contract.parts: part number, ETag, byte count, completion operation, and timestamp are append-only
durability_contract.no_overwrite: one object intent per operation/key plus If-None-Match: * on every direct PUT capability; reissued capabilities remain unable to replace existing bytes
durability_contract.assertions: D1 changes() assertions abort transactions when authority, binding, pending-effect, or terminal postconditions do not hold
